description: Aggregated plot-frame calibration for the 8-chart test corpus. Each chart entry contains the pixel bounding box of the visible plot region ('plot_frame_box', with offset and size), the per-axis pixels-per-coordinate-unit ratios, and the closed-form formulas for converting any (x, y) data coordinate to a (col, row) pixel coordinate in that chart's image.png. A worked example is included for each chart so the formula can be sanity-checked visually.

usage.step_1: Pick a chart by id (e.g. 'el-62'). Open charts['el-62'].
usage.step_2: Read 'plot_frame_box.offset' = top-left corner of the plot in image.png.
usage.step_3: Read 'pixels_per_coordinate_unit.x' and '.y' to know the px/data-unit ratios.
usage.step_4: Use 'data_to_pixel_formula' to convert any (x_value, y_value) into the (col, row) pixel coordinate inside image.png. The 'worked_example' shows the calculation for one known point.

summary_table.title: Parity rate over time by temperature
Maximum parity rate as a function of temperature
Gonotrophic cycle duration at different temperatures
Female fecundity at different temperatures by group
Female survival rate by age and temperature
Daily survival probability by age and temperature
Infective life expectation versus parity rate by temperature

el-60-a.title: Parity rate over time by temperature

plot_frame_box.description: Pixel bounding box of the visible plot region in image.png. Origin (0,0) is the top-left of the image; y grows downward. 'offset' is the top-left corner of the box in image coordinates; 'size' is its width and height in pixels. Detected by finding the y-axis line (left), x-axis line (bottom), and the bounding box of non-white pixels inside the L-shaped quadrant (top, right).

pixels_per_coordinate_unit.description: How many image pixels equal one unit of x or y in data coordinates.

data_to_pixel_formula.explanation: Convert any (x, y) data coordinate to a (col, row) pixel coordinate in image.png. Derived from the linear axis calibration fit during extraction.

worked_example.scenario: Top of the 27°C marker at day 6 (parous-female fraction 0.3611).
worked_example.verification: This (col, row) should land on the green 27°C square at x=6 days.

data_extent_box.description: Pixel bounding box of the labeled data tick range (x=1..23, y=0.0..1.0). For bar charts the bars extend past this box.
